---
title: "LRPGF01: Factors associated with gains in word reading (WR)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF01 is a **gain-factors** model for word reading (`ewrswr`): what is associated
with how much children *gain* across the three period transitions
(t1 $\rightarrow$ t2, t2 $\rightarrow$ t3, t3 $\rightarrow$ t4), modelled as an
ANCOVA — the period post-count given its pre-count — on the logit scale with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood and a child random intercept.

_Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)._ Under the revised study DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised
2026-07-10), **only the randomised on-intervention term is causal.** The
**period-1 average marginal effect** — the genuinely randomised,
all-untreated-baseline transition — is the ITT-anchor estimand; `beta_trt` itself
is only the on-intervention log-odds contrast, not the ITT effect. Every other
coefficient (own baseline, age, cognitive ability, the upstream skills TR, TE, R,
E, L, N and B, and all interactions) is an **adjusted association**: the child
random intercept is a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity
and does **not** control latent general ability (GA), so these internal slopes
remain **descriptive associations**. Report these as "associated with", **never**
"drives". Intervention sessions / dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider).
SES is excluded: it is not a DAG node and was found statistically redundant.

**DAG factor set:** on-intervention (causal) · own baseline · age · cognitive
ability (block design) · taught receptive vocabulary (TR) · taught expressive
vocabulary (TE) · receptive vocabulary (R) · expressive vocabulary (E) · letter
sounds (L) · nonword reading (N) · blending (B) · child random intercept.
**Confounders (adjust_for):** none — word reading has no hearing/speech/phonological-memory
parent in the revised DAG. **Focal interactions:** group $\times$ ability,
group $\times$ own-baseline, age $\times$ ability.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,p} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}[p]}
  + \beta_{\text{trt}}\,\text{OnIntervention}_{i,p}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \sum_{s\in\{TR,TE,R,E,L,N,B\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \textstyle\sum\text{interactions}
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

with $W_{\text{post}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$,
$N = 79$, and $u_{\text{child}} \sim \operatorname{Normal}(0, \sigma_{\text{child}})$.
`OnIntervention` is the immediate arm from period 1 and the waitlist from period 2
(post-crossover). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
